item.title: Example Event
item.description:
Slides can be added in a few ways:

  • Create slides using Wowchemy’s Slides feature and link using slides parameter in the front matter of the talk file
  • Upload an existing slide deck to static/ and link using url_slides parameter in the front matter of the talk file
  • Embed your slides (e.g. Google Slides) or presentation video on this page using shortcodes.

Further event details, including page elements such as image galleries, can be added to the body of this page.

item.title: Cachence: Fine-Grained Cache Partitioning in Both Time and Space
NSFC重点项目
item.description:
项目介绍

本项目为《软件定义的高性能安全异构内存系统结构与技术》（62032001），重点解决异构内存系统在“协同性”“开放性”和“可控性”三个方面的关键科学问题。研究内容涵盖统一局部性理论、异构内存层次协同优化、安全NVM与PIM结构设计、异构设备软件定义方法等，致力于构建支持机器学习、大数据、图计算等新型应用的高效存储系统。通过软硬件协同设计与系统原型验证，项目期望实现存储资源的灵活管理、性能优化与安全保障，推动异构内存系统向更智能、可定制、高性能的方向发展。

论文列表

获奖

人才培养

item.title: Asterinas: A Linux ABI-Compatible, Rust-Based Framekernel OS with a Small and Sound TCB
Files, activities, and more
item.description:
Research Artifacts

Our code repository is hosted on GitHub.

TELOS talks

We organize TELOS talks, inviting speakers from inside and outside the lab to share their research and insights. The conducted and scheduled talks are listed below:

2025-06-27: Scheduler

by Zonghao Zhang

Slides

2025-06-13: Rust in Depth

By Junyang Zhang

Slides

2025-06-06: Introduction to ML Sys

By Zehua Yang

Slides

2025-05-22: Introduction to Fuzzing

By Yonghao Zou

Slides

item.title: Power supply
item.description:
Our lab is grateful for the support from our funding partners and collaborators:

NSFC

Alibaba Group & Ant Group

Huawei

We also acknowledge funding from various other projects and sources. If you are interested in collaborating or supporting our research, please get in touch.

item.title: There will possibly be a spring outing for all TELOS members
item.description: There will possibly be a spring outing for all TELOS members. The destination is still under discussion, but it will be a place with beautiful scenery and rich culture. The travel is expected to take place in months, depending on the weather and travel conditions.

item.title: Criticality-Aware Instruction-Centric Bandwidth Partitioning for Data Center Applications
Aeolia: A Fast and Secure Userspace Interrupt-Based Storage Stack
Blackbox Fuzzing of Distributed Systems with Multi-Dimensional Inputs and Symmetry-Based Feedback Pruning
CortenMM: Efficient Memory Management with Strong Correctness Guarantees
Ginkgo: A Learned Index Enhanced Tiered Memory System
EKRM: Efficient Key-Value Retrieval Method to Reduce Data Lookup Overhead for Redis
Taming Hot Bloat Under Virtualization with HUGESCOPE
Building a High-Performance Graph Storage on Top of Tree-Structured Key-Value Stores
Hardware-Software Collaborative Tiered-Memory Management Framework for Virtualization
Multi-Tenant In-memory Key-Value Cache Partitioning Using Efficient Random Sampling-Based LRU Model
FHPM: Fine-grained Huge Page Management For Virtualization
FLORIA: A Fast and Featherlight Approach for Predicting Cache Performance
vTMM: Tiered Memory Management for Virtual Machines
M3: A Multi-View Fusion and Multi-Decoding Network for Multi-Document Reading Comprehension
Contact
People
People
Tour
Graph Neural Networks Based Memory Inefficiency Detection Using Selective Sampling
Tear Up the Bubble Boom: Lessons Learned From a Deep Learning Research and Development Cluster
Original Content Is All You Need! an Empirical Study on Leveraging Answer Summary for WikiHowQA Answer Selection Task
Exploring GNN based program embedding technologies for binary related tasks
HMM-V: Heterogeneous Memory Management for Virtualization
GSpecPal: Speculation-Centric Finite State Machine Parallelization on GPUs
A Question-Oriented Propagation Network for News Reading Comprehension.
Accelerating Address Translation for Virtualization by Leveraging Hardware Mode
Astraea: A Fair Deep Learning Scheduler for Multi-Tenant GPU Clusters
GRAPHSPY: Fused Program Semantic Embedding through Graph Neural Networks for Memory Efficiency
Efficient Modeling of Random Sampling-Based LRU
Swift shadow paging (SSP): no write-protection but following TLB flushing
Penalty- and Locality-aware Memory Allocation in Redis Using Enhanced AET
Jian Yang and Monica Hall Win the Best Paper Award at Wowchemy 2020
item.description:
Congratulations to Jian Yang and Monica Hall for winning the Best Paper Award at the 2020 Conference on Wowchemy for their paper “Learning Wowchemy”.

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Integer tempus augue non tempor egestas. Proin nisl nunc, dignissim in accumsan dapibus, auctor ullamcorper neque. Quisque at elit felis. Sed nisi tortor, vulputate nec quam non, placerat porta nisl. Nunc varius lobortis urna, condimentum facilisis ipsum molestie eu. Ut molestie eleifend ligula sed dignissim. Duis ut tellus turpis. Praesent tincidunt, nunc sed congue malesuada, mauris enim maximus massa, eget interdum turpis urna et ante. Morbi sem nisl, cursus quis mollis et, interdum luctus augue. Vestibulum tristique pellentesque ex, blandit placerat nunc eleifend sit amet. Fusce eget lectus bibendum, accumsan mi quis, luctus sem. Etiam vitae nulla scelerisque, eleifend odio in, euismod quam. item.title: Richard Hendricks Wins First Place in the Wowchemy Prize
item.description:
Congratulations to Richard Hendricks for winning first place in the Wowchemy Prize.

item.title: GRAPHSPY: Fused Program Semantic-Level Embedding via Graph Neural Networks for Dead Store Detection
Dynamically Configuring LRU Replacement Policy in Redis
Huge Page Friendly Virtualized Memory Management
An example preprint / working paper
item.description:
Create your slides in Markdown - click the Slides button to check out the example.

Add the publication’s full text or supplementary notes here. You can use rich formatting such as including code, math, and images.

item.title: An example journal article
item.description:
Click the Cite button above to demo the feature to enable visitors to import publication metadata into their reference management software.
Create your slides in Markdown - click the Slides button to check out the example.

Add the publication’s full text or supplementary notes here. You can use rich formatting such as including code, math, and images.
